2026-04-20T11:44:11.1525846Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T11:44:11.1526452Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T11:44:11.1526877Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T11:44:11.1527238Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T11:44:11.1527553Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:44:11.1527793Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T11:44:11.1527958Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T11:44:11.1528146Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-20T11:44:11.1528557Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-20T11:44:11.1529299Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-20T11:47:29.3738084Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T11:45:16.853188346Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58382,\"title\":\"[ContainerInstance] Docs: README lacks inline code examples and Key concepts detail after TypeSpec migration\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58067 (commit c767846) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec-based code generation using the new `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter (version 1.4.0-beta.1). The migration introduced and resurfaced several resource types: `ContainerGroup`, `ContainerGroupProfile`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevision`, and `NGroup`. New sample files were added/updated under `tests/Samples/` covering all these resource types.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No inline code examples in README** — The `Examples` section only points to a generic external link (`(aka.ms/redacted) The `tests/Samples/` directory now contains rich auto-generated sample files for `ContainerGroupCollection`, `ContainerGroupResource`, `ContainerGroupProfileCollection`, `ContainerGroupProfileResource`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevisionCollection`, and subscription/resource-group extension methods. None of these are reflected in the README as `Snippet:` placeholders.\\n- **Key concepts section is too minimal** — The section currently contains only a single sentence linking to a generic SDK page. It should describe the primary resource types: `ContainerGroupResource`, `ContainerGroupProfileResource`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevisionResource`, and `NGroupResource`, along with a brief description of each.\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.4.0-beta.1` `Features Added` section is empty** — The migration to the new TypeSpec-based generator changed how APIs are surfaced and introduced new collection result types. Any user-visible behavioral or surface-area changes should be listed.\\n- **Non-standard `Documentation` section in README** — The README includes a `## Documentation` section that is not part of the canonical management-library README template. It should be folded into the standard sections (Getting started / Key concepts / Examples) or removed.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n1. Remove the standalone `## Documentation` section (fold its links into `Getting started` or `Next steps`).\\n\\n2. Replace the `## Key concepts` section with content that describes the primary resource hierarchy:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n\\n### Resource hierarchy\\n\\n- **`ContainerGroupResource`** — Represents an Azure Container Group. Supports create/update, delete, get, list, start/stop/restart, and exec operations.\\n- **`ContainerGroupProfileResource`** — Represents a reusable Container Group Profile (a template for creating container groups). Supports create/update, delete, get, and list operations.\\n- **`ContainerGroupProfileRevisionResource`** — Represents an immutable revision of a Container Group Profile. Supports get and list operations.\\n- **`NGroupResource`** — Represents an N-Group, a resource for managing groups of container instances at scale. Supports create/update, delete, get, list, start/stop, and exec operations.\\n```\\n\\n3. Replace the `## Examples` section so that it includes `Snippet:` placeholders for key scenarios. See Step 2 for the required sample file edits and snippet names.\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a Container Group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get a Container Group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List Container Groups in a Resource Group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Delete a Container Group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/CHANGELOG.md` — **edit**\\n\\nAdd content to the `### Features Added` section under `1.4.0-beta.1`:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Added `NGroupResource` and `NGroupCollection` for managing container instance N-Groups.\\n- Added `ContainerGroupProfileResource`, `ContainerGroupProfileCollection`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevisionResource`, and `ContainerGroupProfileRevisionCollection` for working with reusable container group profile templates and their revisions.\\n- Extended `ContainerGroupResource` with `GetOutboundNetworkDependenciesEndpoints` operation.\\n- `ContainerGroupData` and `ContainerGroupProfileData` now expose additional properties: `ConfigMap`, `StandbyPoolProfile`, and `IsCreatedFromStandbyPool`.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample file at `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/tests/Samples/Sample_HelloWorld.cs` with `#region Snippet:` tags that the `Update-Snippets.ps1` tool can extract into the README.\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/tests/Samples/Sample_HelloWorld.cs` — **create**\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample_HelloWorld\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateContainerGroup()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerInstanceCreateContainerGroup\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerGroupCollection containerGroups = resourceGroup.GetContainerGroups();\\n\\n            ContainerGroupData data = new ContainerGroupData(\\n                new AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;),\\n                new ContainerInstanceContainer[]\\n                {\\n                    new ContainerInstanceContainer(\\u0026#34;mycontainer\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;mcr.microsoft.com/azuredocs/aci-helloworld:latest\\u0026#34;, new ContainerResourceRequirements(\\n                        new ContainerResourceRequestsContent(1.5, 1)))\\n                },\\n                ContainerInstanceOperatingSystemType.Linux);\\n\\n            ArmOperation operation = await containerGroups.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myContainerGroup\\u0026#34;, data);\\n            ContainerGroupResource containerGroup = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Container group created: {containerGroup.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetContainerGroup()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerInstanceGetContainerGroup\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerGroupResource containerGroup = await resourceGroup.GetContainerGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myContainerGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Container group: {containerGroup.Data.Name}, State: {containerGroup.Data.ProvisioningState}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListContainerGroups()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerInstanceListContainerGroups\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            await foreach (ContainerGroupResource containerGroup in resourceGroup.GetContainerGroups())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Container group: {containerGroup.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DeleteContainerGroup()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ContainerInstanceDeleteContainerGroup\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            ContainerGroupResource containerGroup = await resourceGroup.GetContainerGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myContainerGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n            await containerGroup.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine(\\u0026#34;Container group deleted.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe README placeholder blocks (already added in Step 1) must use the snippet names that exactly match the `#region Snippet:` tags above:\\n- `ContainerInstanceCreateContainerGroup`\\n- `ContainerInstanceGetContainerGroup`\\n- `ContainerInstanceListContainerGroups`\\n- `ContainerInstanceDeleteContainerGroup`\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/README.md` must contain these sections in order. Gaps:\\n\\n1. ✅ **Getting started** — present (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client)\\n2. ❌ **Key concepts** — present but only a generic link; needs resource-type descriptions (see Step 1)\\n3. ❌ **Examples** — present but only an external link; needs `Snippet:` placeholders (see Steps 1 \\u0026amp; 2)\\n4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present\\n5. ✅ **Next steps** — present\\n6. ✅ **Contributing** — present\\n7. ⚠️ **Non-standard `Documentation` section** — should be removed or merged into standard sections\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/containerinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerInstance/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 containerinstance` — injects snippet code from `#region Snippet:` tags into README placeholder blocks. The migration introduced and resurfaced several resource types: `ContainerGroup`, `ContainerGroupProfile`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevision`, and `NGroup`. New sample files were added/updated under `tests/Samples/` covering all these resource types.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **No inline code examples in README** — The `Examples` section only points to a generic external link (`(aka.ms/redacted) The `tests/Samples/` directory now contains rich auto-generated sample files for `ContainerGroupCollection`, `ContainerGroupResource`, `ContainerGroupProfileCollection`, `ContainerGroupProfileResource`, `ContainerGroupProfileRevisionCollection`, and subscription/resource-group extension methods. None of these are reflected in the README as `Snippet:` placeholders.\\n- **Key concepts section is too minimal** — The section currently contains only a single sentence linking to a generic SDK page. Supports create/update, delete, get, list, start/stop/restart, and exec operations.\\n- **`ContainerGroupProfileResource`** — Represents a reusable Container Group Profile (a template for creating container groups).
